summary: Verified two SEO fixes: (1) Bing-optimal shortened meta descriptions for home + CR1 (all three tag variants: description/og:description/twitter:description), with CR2/CR3/warranty/find-a-dealer untouched; (2) new /app/frontend/llms.txt source file + _headers block for it. Created /app/backend/tests/test_meta_descriptions_and_llms.py with 44 tests covering file content, source-file sync (DESC_BY_PAGE + build-static ROUTES), old-string absence, live-preview HTTP 200 + content-type text/plain; charset=utf-8, and build idempotency (2× consecutive `node build-static.js` leaves llms.txt untouched). Updated 4 stale tests in older files that hard-coded '15 header blocks' (now 16 with llms.txt) and the CR1 heater-string assertion (description was shortened to condensed dual-voltage form '1kW (120V) or 4kW (240V)' to fit Bing's 120-160 char budget). Full suite: 332/332 pass. Live preview at https://cba431d2-474c-4b3c-8ad3-a929fb18ea97.preview.emergentagent.com/llms.txt returns HTTP 200 + content-type text/plain; charset=utf-8 + body starts with '# California Cooperage'.
critical_code_review_comments: Decoded meta-description lengths: home=153, cr1=141 — both inside Bing's 120-160 optimal range. Note: CR1's decoded length is 141 (not ~155-156 as the review copy suggests); the 156 figure appears to count raw HTML with '"' entities preserved (141 + 3×5-char entities = 156). Either interpretation is compliant.
llms.txt is a first-class source file: build-static.js does NOT emit it and does NOT touch it — idempotency test confirms md5 identical before/after 2 consecutive builds. Good separation of concerns.
_headers now emits 16 path blocks (was 15). Cache-Control on /llms.txt is 3600s with must-revalidate + explicit Content-Type text/plain; charset=utf-8, matching the /robots.txt block pattern.

Live preview served with 'cache-control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate' (from the preview infra, overriding the emitted 1h cache header). Production CDN will honor the 1h cache once files are deployed. Non-blocking for testing.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_headers_file.py (15→16 block count with docstring updated to include /llms.txt)

/app/backend/tests/test_heater_spec_updates.py (2 tests updated: CR1 description now asserts condensed dual-voltage form '1kW (120V) or 4kW (240V)' instead of verbose spec-table string; header block count 15→16)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression is green (332 tests across 8 files). New test file test_meta_descriptions_and_llms.py covers FIX1+FIX2 and doubles as regression for H1/offers/redirects/sitemap. Header block count has moved from 15 to 16 across all 5 test files — any future change (adding/removing a _headers block) needs to update all 5 references OR consolidate them into a single helper. Consider factoring the block-count constant into a shared conftest fixture. llms.txt is source-of-truth at /app/frontend/llms.txt (NOT generated by build-static.js); do not delete it during rebuilds.
rca of the issue: N/A — both SEO fixes verified end-to-end. Home meta description = 153 decoded chars (was 226, target 154), CR1 = 141 decoded chars (was 198, target ~155 raw / 141 visible), all inside Bing's 120-160 visible range. Idempotency confirmed via subprocess: 2 consecutive `node build-static.js` invocations return exit 0 and leave llms.txt md5 unchanged.
